# Achords

> A lightweight protocol for multi-agent software collaboration.

Achords is a multi-agent orchestration protocol designed for software development teams. It provides tools for organization-level resource management, repository coordination, AI-powered code review, and knowledge base documentation.

## Status

**Everything is in development.** No product is production-ready. This is an experimental protocol exploring how AI agents can collaborate on software projects.

## Products

### Organization Base (obase)
- Manages GitHub organization-level resources
- Policies, schemas, and topology configuration
- Available as CLI: `npx achords obase`

### Repository Coordination (rcord)
- Multi-agent coordination within repositories
- Work distribution and conflict resolution
- **Status: In development**

### IA on CI (iaci)
- AI-powered code review in CI/CD pipelines
- Automated business logic validation
- **Status: In development**

### KB Web (kbweb)
- Living documentation that evolves with code
- Powered by Quartz v5
- **Status: In development**

## Protocol

Achords uses a shared memory system (`.engram`) and skill-based architecture where each agent loads specialized instructions for their task.

## Links

- GitHub: https://github.com/cxto21/achords
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/achords
- License: MIT
